page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel processors. It features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance utilizes dedicated CPU allocation on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and runs on the x86_64 architecture.

This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 17 Gbps, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The r8ib.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff that reduces hardware complexity and cost for non-graphical, network-dependent tasks. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that require significant RAM capacity without the need for local physical disks or graphics processing units.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring dedicated Intel processors, high RAM density, and Nitro hypervisor integration without local storage.
